title: Province of Cuneo

infobox.value: ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *provincia di Cuneo*; [Piedmontese](/wiki/Piedmontese_language "Piedmontese language"): *provincia ëd Coni*) is a [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Italy "Provinces of Italy") in the [Piedmont](/wiki/Piedmont "Piedmont") region of [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"). To the west, it borders the French [region](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France") of [Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur](/wiki/Provence-Alpes-C%C3%B4te_d%27Azur "Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur") ([departments](/wiki/D%C3%A9partement "Département") of [Alpes-Maritimes](/wiki/Alpes-Maritimes "Alpes-Maritimes"), [Alpes-de-Haute-Provence](/wiki/Alpes-de-Haute-Provence "Alpes-de-Haute-Provence") and [Hautes-Alpes](/wiki/Hautes-Alpes "Hautes-Alpes")), to the north the [Metropolitan City of Turin](/wiki/Metropolitan_City_of_Turin "Metropolitan City of Turin"), to the east the [province of Asti](/wiki/Province_of_Asti "Province of Asti") and to the south the [Ligurian](/wiki/Liguria "Liguria") provinces of [Savona](/wiki/Province_of_Savona "Province of Savona") and [Imperia](/wiki/Province_of_Imperia "Province of Imperia"). It is also known as *la Provincia Granda* (Piedmontese for 'The Big Province'), because it is the largest province in Piedmont and the fourth-largest in Italy (following [Sassari](/wiki/Province_of_Sassari "Province of Sassari"), [South Tyrol](/wiki/South_Tyrol "South Tyrol") and [Foggia](/wiki/Province_of_Foggia "Province of Foggia")).[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEBole201182-2) [Briga Marittima](/wiki/La_Brigue "La Brigue") and [Tenda](/wiki/Tende "Tende") were part of this province before their cession to France in 1947.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The **province of Cuneo** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *provincia di Cuneo*; [Piedmontese](/wiki/Piedmontese_language "Piedmontese language"): *provincia ëd Coni*) is a [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Italy "Provinces of Italy") in the [Piedmont](/wiki/Piedmont "Piedmont") region of [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"). To the west, it borders the French [region](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France") of [Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur](/wiki/Provence-Alpes-C%C3%B4te_d%27Azur "Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur") ([departments](/wiki/D%C3%A9partement "Département") of [Alpes-Maritimes](/wiki/Alpes-Maritimes "Alpes-Maritimes"), [Alpes-de-Haute-Provence](/wiki/Alpes-de-Haute-Provence "Alpes-de-Haute-Provence") and [Hautes-Alpes](/wiki/Hautes-Alpes "Hautes-Alpes")), to the north the [Metropolitan City of Turin](/wiki/Metropolitan_City_of_Turin "Metropolitan City of Turin"), to the east the [province of Asti](/wiki/Province_of_Asti "Province of Asti") and to the south the [Ligurian](/wiki/Liguria "Liguria") provinces of [Savona](/wiki/Province_of_Savona "Province of Savona") and [Imperia](/wiki/Province_of_Imperia "Province of Imperia"). It is also known as *la Provincia Granda* (Piedmontese for 'The Big Province'), because it is the largest province in Piedmont and the fourth-largest in Italy (following [Sassari](/wiki/Province_of_Sassari "Province of Sassari"), [South Tyrol](/wiki/South_Tyrol "South Tyrol") and [Foggia](/wiki/Province_of_Foggia "Province of Foggia")).[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEBole201182-2) [Briga Marittima](/wiki/La_Brigue "La Brigue") and [Tenda](/wiki/Tende "Tende") were part of this province before their cession to France in 1947.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Province_of_Cuneo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Economy")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/c/cc/Ferrero_Headquarter_%28Nutella_company%29.jpg/250px-Ferrero_Headquarter_%28Nutella_company%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ferrero_Headquarter_(Nutella_company).jpg)

Headquarters of [Ferrero SpA](/wiki/Ferrero_SpA "Ferrero SpA") in [Alba](/wiki/Alba,_Piedmont "Alba, Piedmont")

Companies active in the province include:

* [Michelin](/wiki/Michelin "Michelin") in [Mondovì](/wiki/Mondov%C3%AC "Mondovì")
* [Miroglio](/wiki/Miroglio "Miroglio") in [Alba](/wiki/Alba,_Piedmont "Alba, Piedmont")
* [Ferrero SpA](/wiki/Ferrero_SpA "Ferrero SpA") in Alba
* Maina in [Fossano](/wiki/Fossano "Fossano")
* Balocco in Fossano
* [Merlo](/wiki/Merlo_(company) "Merlo (company)") in San Defendente ([Cervasca](/wiki/Cervasca "Cervasca"))
* Arpa industriale in [Bra](/wiki/Bra,_Piedmont "Bra, Piedmont")
* Bottero in [Cuneo](/wiki/Cuneo "Cuneo")
* [Mondo](/wiki/Mondo_(Italian_company) "Mondo (Italian company)") in Alba
* Mtm-Brc in [Cherasco](/wiki/Cherasco "Cherasco")
* Abet in Bra
* Edizioni San Paolo in Alba

Many important industrial groups have branches in the province: [Michelin](/wiki/Michelin "Michelin") (Cuneo and Fossano), [Saint-Gobain](/wiki/Saint-Gobain "Saint-Gobain") ([Savigliano](/wiki/Savigliano "Savigliano")), [Valeo](/wiki/Valeo "Valeo") ([Mondovì](/wiki/Mondov%C3%AC "Mondovì")), [Asahi Glass Co.](/wiki/Asahi_Glass_Co. "Asahi Glass Co.") (Cuneo), [ITT](/wiki/ITT_Inc. "ITT Inc.") ([Barge](/wiki/Barge,_Piedmont "Barge, Piedmont")), [Diageo](/wiki/Diageo "Diageo") ([Santa Vittoria d'Alba](/wiki/Santa_Vittoria_d%27Alba "Santa Vittoria d'Alba")) and [Nestlé](/wiki/Nestl%C3%A9 "Nestlé") ([Moretta](/wiki/Moretta "Moretta")).[[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEHolst-WarhaftSteenhuis201276-5)

Cuneo is also the land of important wines, mostly produced in the [Langhe](/wiki/Langhe "Langhe") and [Roero](/wiki/Roero "Roero") hills, such as [Barbaresco](/wiki/Barbaresco "Barbaresco"), [Barolo](/wiki/Barolo "Barolo"), [Nebbiolo](/wiki/Nebbiolo "Nebbiolo"), [Barbera](/wiki/Barbera_d%27Asti "Barbera d'Asti") and many others.
